executive_brief: The Expense Tracker is a client-side personal finance application designed for single-user expense logging. It implements a CRUD-lite pattern (Create, Read, Delete) using browser localStorage for persistence, featuring a summary dashboard with real-time totals and a date-descending transaction history.

architectural_constraints: Amount must be a positive number > 0
Dashboard display limit: Most recent 50 expenses by default
Accessibility: Zero critical WCAG AA violations
Data Persistence: LocalStorage only (no backend/cloud sync)
User Access: No authentication, single-user local session
Functional Exclusion: No edit/update capability in v1 (Delete and Re-add only)

maturity_assessment: The specifications are high-quality and logically consistent, with a strong mapping between user stories and functional requirements. The project is READY for execution, although a formal 'Scope & Out-of-Scope' section is missing; however, this is mitigated by the explicit exclusion of editing and authentication within the requirements.
critical_dependencies: Browser LocalStorage API for data persistence
Strict relational binding between Expense entity and the predefined Category list
Real-time total calculation trigger upon Expense addition or deletion
WCAG AA compliance gate for dashboard UI
